task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to cameochemicals.noaa.gov.

task_completion.description: Search for and view chemical data on CAMEO Chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the CAMEO chemical detail pages for Methane, Propane, Hydrogen, or Acetone, with evidence of looking for the Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) under the NIOSH guidelines section.

task_completion.description: Open a text editor or terminal to create the output file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) or terminal is open, showing the creation or editing of a file named 'alarm_setpoints.txt' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.
The text file contains the exact header row: 'Chemical Name | LEL (%) | Setpoint (ppm)'.

task_completion.description: Record correct calculations for Methane and Propane
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the rows for Methane and Propane with correct values. Look for 'Methane | 5.0 | 5000' and 'Propane | 2.1 | 2100' (minor spacing variations around pipes are acceptable).

task_completion.description: Record correct calculations for Hydrogen and Acetone
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the rows for Hydrogen and Acetone with correct values. Look for 'Hydrogen | 4.0 | 4000' and 'Acetone | 2.5 | 2500' (Note: 'Acetone | 2.6 | 2600' is also acceptable due to a known discrepancy).

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the CAMEO Chemicals website
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing in the search bar, clicking links, or navigating the CAMEO website GUI, rather than bypassing the UI with background scripts.

integrity.description: Agent retrieved data from the specified source rather than hallucinating
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must be seen actually viewing the chemical detail pages on CAMEO Chemicals to find the LEL values, rather than instantly generating the text file from its pre-trained knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent manually constructed the output file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or constructing the contents of 'alarm_setpoints.txt' in a text editor or via terminal echo/cat commands, rather than the completed file appearing instantaneously without visible creation steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected LEL and Setpoint values: Methane (LEL: 5.0%, Setpoint: 5000 ppm), Propane (LEL: 2.1%, Setpoint: 2100 ppm), Hydrogen (LEL: 4.0%, Setpoint: 4000 ppm), Acetone (LEL: 2.5% or 2.6%, Setpoint: 2500 or 2600 ppm). The output file must be located at /home/ga/Documents/alarm_setpoints.txt and use a pipe-delimited format with the exact header 'Chemical Name | LEL (%) | Setpoint (ppm)'.
